free_text: Cross sections from proton irradiation of thorium at
800 MeV
Details of the target
Experiment performed using Target 2
Blue Room of the Weapons Neutron Research Facility
at LANSCE
Natural thorium foils of 99.7% purity were used. Two
foils were used as targets for this experiment, each
measuring 2.5cm x 2.5 cm and having thicknesses of
48.1 and 52.8 mg/cm2. Aluminum beam monitoring
foil with the same dimensions and a thickness of
65.3mg/cm2 was incorporated downstream of the thorium
targets. All foils were sandwiched between single
layers of Kapton tape with a thickness of 25 mu-m,
which served as a catcher foil for recoil ions.
The high-purity germanium (HPGe) detector used
to assay the foils was a p-type aluminum windowed
ORTEC GEM detector with a relative efficiency at 1333
keV of about 10% and a measured gamma peak FWHM at
1333 keV of 1.99 keV.
The activity at the end of bombardment of each
isotope of interest was determined by the fitting of
its decay curve, and cross sections were calculated
using the activation formula relative to monitor
cross section.
Total uncertainty obtained by summing in
quadrature following uncertainties
Uncertainty in detector efficiency
Uncertainty in counting geometry
Uncertainty in branching ratio
and peak area
Compiled by S.H.

Half-life 0.16 days given in Table I of the article is
typographical error. Author confirmed that correct
value of 3.85 days was used.
